novel_features.name: Cross-board activity since window
novel_features.description: Tail every change across every synced board since a window or timestamp; filter by user, column, or board without burning API points.
novel_features.rationale: Joins the local activity_logs table with a thin API tail-fetch since the cached high-water mark; the web UI's universal Activity view caps at the latest ~50 events and the per-board API requires separate calls per board.
novel_features.why_it_matters: When an agent needs to answer 'what changed since standup' or 'what did this user touch today' across multiple boards, this is the only command that returns a single ranked stream without N round-trips.

novel_features.name: Per-person workload across boards
novel_features.description: Per-user open-item count weighted by status and overdue flag, computed across every board the user is assigned on.
novel_features.rationale: SQLite join across items + person-typed column_values + boards + status that no single GraphQL call returns.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers 'who's overloaded this sprint?' and 'what is my real plate?' in one call instead of filtering each board by Person column.

novel_features.name: Status-bottleneck dwell-time analyzer
novel_features.description: Per-status median and p90 dwell time per item, computed from activity-log transitions on a board.
novel_features.rationale: Reads activity_logs locally and reconstructs per-item status transitions; the API exposes raw activity entries but never the dwell-time aggregation.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Surfaces the actual bottleneck status ("In Review" sat 9 days p90) when an agent is asked why velocity dropped.

novel_features.name: Sprint velocity & slip report
novel_features.description: Per-sprint committed / completed / slipped / added-mid-sprint counts, plus per-person delivered, across the last N sprint cycles.
novel_features.rationale: Joins sprint metadata API + local activity log + items; the official sprint summary tool reports headline numbers but not slip or added-mid-sprint.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Replaces the "export to CSV every Friday and run three SQL queries" loop for engineering managers.

novel_features.name: Column-schema drift detector
novel_features.description: Reports columns added, removed, renamed, or retyped on a board since the last sync.
novel_features.rationale: Diffs cached boards.columns JSON against a fresh get_board_info response; the polymorphic typed column_values are the central pain point for tooling and a single column-type swap breaks downstream pipelines silently.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Catches "someone changed status to dropdown" before downstream automations break with no warning.

novel_features.name: Cross-board contextual mentions
novel_features.description: FTS5 search across item names, update bodies, doc bodies, and text-typed column values; result rows hydrated with board, group, and owner context.
novel_features.rationale: The base search command finds matches; mentions returns context-rich rows so an agent can answer "where is Acme Corp mentioned?" with one call instead of joining N results.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Cross-board reverse-lookup that the official universal search exposes only in the web UI and never as structured agent output.

novel_features.name: Pre-flight complexity budget
novel_features.description: Predicts the complexity-points cost of a query and the remaining account-minute budget without executing the body.
novel_features.rationale: GraphQL exposes a `complexity { before after reset_in_x_seconds }` field on every operation, but no existing tool surfaces it as a budget probe. Without this, complexity-points are unobservable until the call fails.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent answer "can I run this bulk read right now?" before it gets a 429 mid-loop.

novel_features.name: Bulk column-value editor with typed dry-run
novel_features.description: Read a CSV; validate every cell against the cached typed-column schema; print a unified diff in dry-run; on apply, run change_item_column_values per row with per-row exit codes and resume-on-failure.
novel_features.rationale: The absorbed items-set --stdin command pipes raw values; bulk-edit adds CSV-shaped input, schema validation, and a true diff so the user can stage hundreds of edits safely.
novel_features.why_it_matters: RevOps integrators currently re-build dry-run themselves in Python every time; this is the safety net that makes Monday a first-class CSV-driven backend.

novel_features.name: Mirror & formula dependency resolver
novel_features.description: For a given item or column, walks mirror and formula chains in the local store and prints the source board, source item, source column, and final resolved value.
novel_features.rationale: Mirror columns hide their source; formula columns hide their inputs; the schema knows the linkage but no public tool exposes the chain.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Answers "why is this column showing this value?" without clicking through the source board manually.

novel_features.name: External-CSV reconcile
novel_features.description: Joins a local board to an external CSV by a chosen column-value key; emits only-in-monday, only-in-csv, and diff sets.
novel_features.rationale: RevOps glue between Monday and Salesforce/Stripe currently re-implements this in Python every time.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Mid-week reconcile becomes a one-liner instead of a script.

novel_features.name: Per-board health scorecard
novel_features.description: Per-board scorecard: % items with owner, % with due-date, % overdue, % updated in last 7d, count of empty status, count of broken mirror columns.
novel_features.rationale: Pure local store aggregation; the API has no equivalent rollup.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Friday status snapshot becomes a single command per board.

novel_features.name: Item full-context dump
novel_features.description: For one item-id, returns one JSON blob with the item, every column-value typed, every update, every reply, every linked doc, every asset metadata, every activity log entry, and every mirror source.
novel_features.rationale: A single SQLite join over 8 local tables that no single API call covers; ideal for piping to an LLM as task context.
novel_features.why_it_matters: One command to give an agent everything about one item without N round-trips.

novel_features.name: Cross-system ID exporter
novel_features.description: Joins Monday items to whichever column stores their cross-system ID (Linear id, Notion page-id, Slack thread-ts) and emits a structured list ready to pipe into another tool.
novel_features.rationale: Teams that bridge Monday with Linear, Notion, or Slack store the cross-system ID in a column on the Monday item. The local store already has every column value typed; this command makes the join key explicit and the JSON output the contract for downstream CLIs.
novel_features.why_it_matters: Lets an agent answer 'which Monday items map to which Linear issues?' in one call, then pipe straight into linear-pp-cli without writing glue.

narrative.headline: Every monday.com feature, plus offline SQL, FTS5 search, typed column-value handling, and bulk edits no other monday.com tool has.
narrative.value_prop: Mirrors monday.com's GraphQL API as ~40 typed commands, then transcends with a local SQLite store, FTS5 search across items/updates/docs/columns, complexity-aware retry, and 12 commands that only work because everything is in the store: cross-board activity windows, per-person workload, sprint slip reports, status-bottleneck dwell time, column-schema drift, contextual mentions, complexity-budget pre-flight, typed bulk edits with dry-run, mirror/formula resolution, CSV reconcile, board health, and item full-context dumps.
narrative.auth_narrative: monday.com requires an API token from your account's Developer page. Set it via `MONDAY_API_TOKEN` and the CLI sends it as the `Authorization` header (no Bearer prefix). Tokens inherit the issuing user's permissions, so private boards your user can't see remain invisible to the CLI.

quickstart.comment: Confirm MONDAY_API_TOKEN is set and you're authenticated as the right user.
Probe auth, reachability, and the current complexity budget so you know how many points the next sync will cost.
Pull every workspace, board, group, item, column, update, and activity-log row into the local SQLite mirror.
Cross-column search on a board for items whose name (or named column) matches the term.
What changed in the last two hours on the sprint board, ranked.
Stage a typed column-value edit without sending it; flip --dry-run off to apply.

troubleshoots.fix: Run `monday-pp-cli complexity-budget --query <file>` first; if predicted points exceed the per-call 5M cap, narrow the field selection or paginate.
monday.com tokens are sent raw in the `Authorization` header, no Bearer prefix. Re-issue via developer.monday.com -> Developer -> My Tokens and run `monday-pp-cli auth set-token`.

troubleshoots.symptom: items list returns 25 rows when the board has thousands
troubleshoots.fix: items list auto-paginates; if you see only one page check that --limit is not capped, and use `monday-pp-cli sync --board <id>` to land all rows in the local store.

troubleshoots.symptom: column_value JSON looks different than yesterday's run
troubleshoots.fix: An admin probably retyped the column. Run `monday-pp-cli column-drift --board <id>` to see exactly which columns changed shape since last sync.

narrative.when_to_use: Reach for monday-pp-cli when an agent task needs to answer cross-board questions, mutate many items at once with a safe dry-run, or replay activity that the official MCP can't return in a single call. Prefer it over the official MCP when the answer requires joining multiple resources or running offline; prefer the official MCP for one-shot reads of a single resource.

recipes.title: Find every item that mentions a customer across all your boards
recipes.explanation: Cross-column substring search over items + (with --updates) recent comment bodies on every supplied board. Output rows include the board name, group title, item name, which field matched, and a context-rich snippet.

recipes.title: Replay everything a teammate touched today
recipes.explanation: Activity-log tail-fetch on the supplied boards, filtered to one user. Each row is a board with its activity_logs array; events include column-value changes, item creates, and moves.

recipes.title: Stage a 200-row status update from a Salesforce export
recipes.explanation: Reads the CSV, parses each row into a typed column-value plan, and prints what would change without sending. Flip to --apply once the plan looks right.

recipes.title: Show me sprint slip from the last four cycles
recipes.explanation: Combines current per-status item counts with activity-log status transitions over the last few pages: how many items entered Done vs left Done, plus a snapshot of what's where right now.

recipes.title: Get every piece of context an LLM might need about one item
recipes.explanation: One SQLite join over items + column_values + updates + replies + docs + assets + activity_logs + mirror sources; the agent gets everything in a single call.

recipes.title: Bridge Monday items to your Linear board
recipes.command: monday-pp-cli cross-ref --board sprint-q3 --link-column linear_id --json | jq '.[] | select(.linker != null)'
recipes.explanation: Joins Monday items to whichever column stores the Linear issue id; the output JSON is the contract for piping into linear-pp-cli without writing glue.

trigger_phrases: find this in monday across boards
